questionText: API latency is variable & app is sluggish. How do you design UI safely?

whatWorkedWell.text: Great structured thinking — broke the problem into latency, retry, and fallback cleanly.
Mentioned skeleton loaders and optimistic updates — shows real product sense.
Discussed trade-offs between consistency and availability clearly.
Good use of caching strategies (stale-while-revalidate) for API responses.

overallTakeaways.text: Quantify your answers — mention P99 latencies, SLA targets to sound more concrete.
Discuss observability — how would you monitor and alert on degradation?
Consider edge cases — what happens when all fallbacks fail simultaneously?
Mention circuit breakers and bulkhead patterns for resilience engineering.

keyMoments.description: Introduced the concept of graceful degradation early on, showing structured problem decomposition.
Paused to think about retry mechanisms and exponential backoff strategies.
Confidently explained skeleton vs spinner trade-offs with real product examples.
Discussed stale-while-revalidate pattern for API response caching effectively.
Clearly articulated trade-offs between consistency and availability in distributed systems.
